CYBERSECURITY

How Small Businesses Can Stay Safe in a Digital World

By Little Fight NYC · Published January 15, 2025

Updated March 20, 2026 · 5 min read

Security basics Payments + devices Small-team practicalSAFE

Running a small business is a juggling act. Customers, staff, the register, the books. The last thing you need is a hacker in the mix.

Here’s the scary stat: over half of small businesses have no security measures at all. Most owners figure they’re too small to be a target. Hackers love that assumption.

The good news: you can fight back. Here are the steps that matter, in plain words.

1. Guard Every Credit Card Transaction

Small businesses live on payments. Card swipes, taps, online checkouts. Keeping them secure keeps your customers’ trust — and your money.

If you use point-of-sale devices, make sure they're using EMV chip readers or contactless payments, which are far safer than old magnetic stripe swipes. And beware of skimmers or tampering—regularly inspect card readers for anything suspicious. Shockingly, 27% of small businesses with no cybersecurity protections still collect customers' credit card info, leaving both the business and customers exposed.

Don’t be the statistic. Lock down the payment path so nobody can skim cards or grab payments in transit. Your customers will thank you. So will your books.

How Little Fight NYC Can Help: Secure transactions don't have to be complicated. We consult on POS system setup to ensure you're using up-to-date, secure payment tech. From choosing a safe payment processor to configuring your in-store Wi-Fi for secure transactions, our team has you covered.

2. Use Industry-Standard Protection for Websites and POS Systems

Got a website or an iPad register? Make sure both use standard safeguards. That means encryption, updates, and real passwords.

For websites, use trusted platforms and keep them updated. For point-of-sale, stick to the big names and turn on their security features.

Turn on the PCI compliance features too. Good POS and e-commerce systems walk you through it. It’s the card industry’s basic safety checklist.

Feeling buried in tech? You don’t have to do this alone. We translate. We set it up. You get back to work.

3. Lock Down Devices and Accounts (Local Security)

Count the devices that run your business. The laptop. The register tablet. Everyone’s phones. Each one is a door — and every door needs a lock.

Change every default password. No admin/admin. Update the router. And clean up old accounts — that ex-employee login is an open door.

Turn on multi-factor authentication everywhere you can. That’s the text code or app prompt at login. One extra tap, and it stops most break-ins cold.

And if all this sounds like a lot to manage, don't worry. We routinely help small businesses implement account-level protections. Little Fight NYC can assist in setting up password managers for your team and configuring MFA on your critical accounts—keeping the keys to the kingdom firmly in safe hands.

4. Protect Your People and Personal Devices (Passwords & Phishing)

The best tech won’t help if someone gets tricked into opening the door. Security is a people thing as much as a tech thing.

Phishing is still the #1 way small businesses get breached — 96% of phishing arrives by email, and small firms get hit hardest. Teach your team to slow down and check the sender.

Use a password manager. It ends password recycling, and nobody has to remember thirty logins.

How we help: short, no-jargon training. How to spot a scam. What to do when something feels off. No lectures.

5. Shield Your Website with Updates, SSL, and Login Protection

Your website is your digital storefront. It needs a lock on the door, same as your shop. Attacks on small-business sites happen every day.

  • Turn on HTTPS. That’s the padlock in the address bar. It scrambles data between your site and your customers.
  • Keep the software updated. WordPress, plugins, whatever you run. Updates patch the holes hackers use.
  • Protect the admin login. Strong password, MFA, and limit the login attempts. Bots try millions of combos — make yours a dead end.

      • Do these things and your site becomes a hard target. Most hackers give up and go find an easier one.

      Stay Safe Out There—We've Got Your Back

      The internet can feel like the Wild West. You don’t have to police it alone. Threats change — having a partner beats guessing.

      Still feels like a lot? That’s what we’re for. Helping small businesses fight the digital bullies is the whole mission.

      Staying safe isn’t a one-time task. It’s a little fight you take on every day. Stay alert, stay updated, and ask for help when you need it.

      After all, your business is worth protecting—and together, we'll make sure the only people getting in are the ones you want in (like your wonderful customers). Stay safe out there, and remember: you've got this, and we've got your back every step of the way!

      Need Help Securing Your Business?

      We can help you implement these security measures and more.
